text: Mother. The synagogue continues to pray for Christ's coming. (Worthington)<hr>Her request has been heard, and He has assumed our nature; so that we may receive from him all the marks of tenderness, and none but the carnal will shew any signs of contempt. The spouse wishes to manifest her love without restraint, and to be delivered from the shame of sterility, (Calmet) or the Church desires to see Christ in heaven, and to praise him. (Menochius)

Teach me economy, on which subject Xenophon represents the newly married entertaining themselves.<hr>Wine. Mark 15:23., and Osee 14:8. Nectar was of this description, (Calmet) being composed of wine, honey, and odoriferous flowers. The best was made at Babylon. (Athen. 1:25., and 2:2.)<hr>These liquors intimate all the virtues, which Christ has taught; the practice of which affords him the greatest satisfaction, John 14:23. They are best learnt in retirement, (Calmet) and from our great teacher, Micheas 4:2. (Menochius)

Jerusalem. Septuagint add, "by the armies and powers of the field," as [in] Canticles 2:7., and 3:5. (Haydock)<hr>The spouse presently awakes after the sixth night, and goes out. (Calmet)<hr>Christ admonishes all not to disturb those who would serve him. (Worthington)

Flowing. Thus speak the companions of the spouse; and the bridegroom, turning to her alone, reminds her of an adventure. The Jews would put this in the mouth of the spouse. (Calmet)<hr>Corrupted. Hebrew and Septuagint, "fell in labour, there she was delivered who bore thee." (Haydock)<hr>Christ redeemed Eve, who had been seduced to eat the forbidden fruit, and by his sacred blood on the cross, awoke and healed our fallen nature. (Calmet)<hr>The court of heaven admires the Church of the Gentiles, and every pious soul ascending from this world, particularly the blessed Virgin [Mary]. The synagogue was corrupt, when she called for the death of the Son of God, and said, His blood be upon us, etc. [Matthew 27:25.] We have no king but Caesar. [John 19:15.] (Worthington)<hr>Grace must go before, that our free-will may also walk forward. (Menochius)

Arm. Working by charity, Galatians 5:6. (Haydock)<hr>Women in the east imprint what they like most on their bosom and arms, or carry bracelets and gems on those parts, with similar inscriptions, Osee 2:2., and Isaias 3:20. (St. Clement, Paed. 2:11.)<hr>Christ must live in us. (Calmet)<hr>Tota ejus species exprimatur in nobis. (St. Ambrose, Isaac viii.)<hr>Jealousy. That is, zealous and burning love. (Challoner)<hr>Hell. Or the grave, which subdues all. Love is a violent passion. (Calmet)<hr>Nullus liber erit, si quis amare velit. (Prop. ii.)<hr>Christ gave his life to redeem mankind, and the martyrs have joyfully endured torments and death, to evince their love. (Calmet)<hr>Flames. Hebrew, "a flame of God," or most vehement. (Haydock)

Drown it. As other fires may be extinguished. He who sinks under persecution, has not real charity. Temptation does not weaken a person, but shews what he is, Ecclesiasticus 34:9. (Calmet)<hr>He shall. Worldlings will ridicule his parting with temporal delights, for those which do not appear: but the true lover will make no account of the former. (Calmet)

Our sister. Christ styles the Church his own and the synagogue's sister, promising her many benefits. (Worthington)<hr>The relations of the spouse wish to have her married, though she seemed young, Ezechiel 16:7.<hr>Spoken to, concerning marriage, or its consummation, Genesis 34:6., and Daniel 13:57. (Calmet)<hr>The synagogue had but few to give her proper instructions. (Menochius)<hr>But Christ would provide for her, if she were not wanting to herself. (Haydock)

Cedar. Procuring for her a rich and steady husband to protect her. These are the words of the bridegroom. The Christian Church seemed weak at first. But her pastors and martyrs caused her to triumph over all the powers of hell. (Calmet)<hr>Those who correspond with the first grace, are furnished with others. (Menochius)
The. The bridegroom, in the character of a countryman, asserts that he would not give his vineyard or spouse (Calmet) for all the king's riches.

Before me. I am satisfied with my spouse.<hr>The peaceable. Hebrew and Septuagint, "Solomon." (Haydock)<hr>Retain them, and also pay the keepers their wages. I envy not all the treasures of the world; nor would put them in competition with my vineyard, or spouse. The synagogue of Moses and Solomon degenerated, while the Church of Christ has continued faithful, and has always some good workmen. (St. Gregory; Ven. Bede, etc.) (Calmet)

Hearken. The seventh morning is come, and I must depart to the mountains, Canticles 2:17., and 4:6. Christ, before his ascension, exhorts his Church to present her petitions. He is always ready to hear her, and to grant her aid, in every emergency. See St. Ambrose, de Virg. 16:99.<hr>The virtuous pray that her requests may be granted, (Haydock) and are eager to know what they are. (Menochius)

Flee. The Church consents that her beloved should ascend to heaven, as he still remains with her. (Ven. Bede; St. Bernard, ser. ix.) Qui habitat. (Calmet)<hr>The whole Church militant requests that he would ascend thither, for the good of all his servants, begging for an abundant supply of grace, that we may ascend the high mountains of perfect charity, and zeal for God's honour; and that he would make our souls such hills and gardens, adorned with all the flowers and fruits of virtue, in which he may vouchsafe to dwell. Amen. (Worthington)<hr>Flee to heaven, and draw me with thee, Canticles 1:4. (Menochius)
